title: Methods for producing metal powders
abstract: A method for producing a metal powder includes maintaining molten reducing metal in a sealed reaction vessel that is substantially free of oxygen and water, establishing a vortex in the molten reducing metal, introducing a metal halide into the vortex so that the molten reducing metal is in a stoichiometric excess to the metal halide, thereby producing metal particles and salt, removing unreacted reducing metal, removing the salt, and recovering the metal powder. The molten reducing metal can be a Group I metal, a Group II metal, or aluminum.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for producing a metal powder, the method comprising: a) maintaining molten reducing metal in a sealed reaction vessel that is substantially free of oxygen and water, the molten reducing metal comprising a Group I metal, a Group II metal, or aluminum; b) establishing a vortex in the molten reducing metal; c) introducing a metal halide into the vortex so that the molten reducing metal is in a stoichiometric excess to the metal halide, thereby producing metal particles and a salt; d) removing unreacted reducing metal; e) removing the salt; and f) recovering the metal powder. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the metal halide includes a chloride of tantalum, nickel, aluminum, zirconium, vanadium, tin, titanium, silicon, niobium, and hafnium. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the metal halide is a mixture of metal halides. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the vortex is created by stirring the molten reducing metal with a bladed stirrer in the reaction vessel at a speed of about 500 rpm to about 6000 rpm. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the vortex is created by stirring the molten reducing metal with a bladed stirrer in the reaction vessel at a tip speed of about 4000 feet per minute to about 6000 feet per minute. 6. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises sodium. 7. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises potassium. 8. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises sodium and potassium. 9. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises magnesium. 10. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises calcium. 11. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal comprises aluminum. 12. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal is maintained at a temperature of about 150Â°C to about 850Â°C. 13. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal is maintained at a temperature of about 150Â°C to about 350Â°C. 14. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal is maintained at a temperature of about 200Â°C to about 250Â°C. 15. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal is maintained at a temperature of about 600Â°C to about 700Â°C. 16. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the molten reducing metal is in at least a 5: 1 stoichiometric excess to the metal halide. 17. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the reaction vessel that is substantially free of oxygen and water is filled or purged with an inert gas prior to or while maintaining the molten reducing metal in the sealed reaction vessel. 18. The method of claims 1-5, wherein removing the unreacted reducing metal comprises siphoning off the unreacted reducing metal. 19. The method of claims 1-5, wherein removing the unreacted molten reducing metal comprises evaporating the unreacted reducing metal. 20. The method of claims 1-5, wherein removing the unreacted reducing metal comprises reacting the unreacted reducing metal with an anhydrous chloride. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the anhydrous chloride is anhydrous hydrogen chloride or chloride gas. 22. The method of claims 1-5, wherein removing the salt comprises washing the metal particles with water. 23. The method of claims 1-5, wherein removing the salt comprises evaporating by heating and sweeping with an inert gas. 24. The method of claims 1-5, further comprising drying the metal powder after recovering the metal powder. 25. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the metal powder is an alloy. 26. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the metal halide comprises a powder. 27. The method of claims 1-5, wherein the metal halide comprises a liquid.
